chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمِ بَعْدَ اغْتِسَالِهِ مِنَ الطِّيبِ وَ الصَّيْدِ وَ غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُلَبِّيَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 79 – What is allowed for the one in Ihraam after his washing, from the perfume, and the hunting, and other than that, before he exclaims Talbiyya

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/256)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/256)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا تَقُولُ فِي رَجُلٍ تَهَيَّأَ لِلْإِحْرَامِ وَ فَرَغَ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ الصَّلَاةِ وَ جَمِيعِ الشُّرُوطِ إِلَّا أَنَّهُ لَمْ يُلَبِّ أَ لَهُ أَنْ يَنْقُضَ ذَلِكَ وَ يُوَاقِعَ النِّسَاءَ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ismail Bin Marrar, from Yunus, from Ziyad Bin Marwan who said,

‘I said to Abu Al Hassanasws, ‘What are youasws saying regarding a man who prepares to (wear) the Ihraam, and is free from performing everything, the Prayer and the entirety of the stipulations, except that he has not exclaimed Talbiyya. Is it for him that he breaks that and falls upon the wife?’ So heasws said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 10. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Isma‘il ibn Marrar from Yunus from Ziyad ibn Marwan who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is ready for Ihram and has completed everything, like Salat (prayer) and all other conditions except Talbiyah; if he can cancel it all for sexual intercourse. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can do so,”’
